0000-Vitruvius-50BC-Ten-Books-of-Archite.title: The Ten Books on Architecture
0000-Vitruvius-50BC-Ten-Books-of-Archite.description: Vitruvius’s classical Roman treatise on architecture and engineering—town planning, temples, the orders, proportion, materials, machines, and civil works. Widely read through later ages as a foundation for Western building theory (here in the Morgan translation with Harvard University Press front matter).

0226869393.pdf.title: Modern Architecture: A Guidebook for His Students to This Field of Art
0226869393.pdf.description: Otto Wagner’s landmark 1896 text (Getty Publications; trans. Harry Francis Mallgrave) criticizes historicist eclecticism and argues for a contemporary architecture suited to modern life. This edition follows the expanded 1902 German text with emendations from the 1896, 1898, and 1914 versions—covering style, composition, construction, and practice, with introductory essays on Wagner’s place in fin‑de‑siècle theory.

03. Architect_s Data.pdf.title: Architects' Data
03. Architect_s Data.pdf.description: Ernst Neufert’s canonical planning reference: standardized dimensions, ergonomics, spatial requirements, and technical layout data used daily in architectural offices—dimensions for housing, public buildings, accessibility, and services (international English edition).

4+1view-architecture.pdf.title: Architectural Blueprints—The “4+1” View
4+1view-architecture.pdf.description: Model of Software Architecture Philippe Kruchten Rational Software Corp. Abstract This article presents a model for describing the architecture of software-intensive systems, based on the use of multiple, concurrent views. This use of multiple views allows to address separately the concerns of the various ‘stakeholders’ of the architecture: end-user, developers, systems engineers, project managers, etc., and to handle separately the functional and non functional requirements. Each of the five views is described, together with a notation to capture it. T…

Kristin-Oretorp_Booklet-curated_ARK258_2.description: Musical mapping in architecture ARK 258 | Matter Space Structure | Kristin Oretorp | 2021 2 Kristin Oretorp | 2021 | MSS3 Music is liquid architecture; architecture is frozen music. Johann Wolfgang von Goethe Sight isolates, whereas sound incorporates; vision is directional, whereas sound is omni-directional. The sense of sight implies exteriority, but sound creates an experience of interiority. I regard an object, but sound approaches me; the eye reaches, but the ear receives. has_fat.pdf.title: ARCHITECTURE FOR THE POOR
has_fat.pdf.description: HASAN FATHY 1 Prelude Dream and Reality Paradise Lost: The Countryside If you were given a million pounds, what would you do with them? A question they were always asking us when we were young, one that would start our imagination roaming and set us daydreaming. I had two possible answers: one, to buy a yacht, hire an orchestra, and sail round the world with my friends listening to Bach, Schumann, and Brahms; the other, to build a village where the fellaheen would follow the way of life that I would like them to.
